e_visa.nationality: Eligible nationalities not specifically enumerated on official source
e_visa.notes: Moldova offers e-visa (Type C short-stay) via evisa.gov.md for nationalities that do not qualify for visa-free entry. Processing: 10 business days. Applications submitted online. Consular fee payable by bank card. Visa sent by email for use at any border crossing point.

visa_policy.notes: 90 days within any 6-month period (from moment of first entry) for visa-free nationals. Footnote [2] countries are exempt only if holding a valid residence permit or visa (excluding transit) issued by an EU Member State, Schengen state, UK, USA, or Canada. Footnote [1] countries (Egypt, Indonesia, Iran, Turkmenistan, Vietnam) are listed on the visa-REQUIRED section of the MFA page - ordinary passports need a visa; only diplomatic/service/special passport holders retain a treaty-based exemption. The e-visa portal (evisa.gov.md) processes short-stay Type C visas electronically. Moldova denounced the 1992 CIS Bishkek Agreement on visa-free travel in November 2025; this affects Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan (no bilateral agreements), though the MFA page still shows them as visa-free as of June 2026. Morocco is visa-REQUIRED for ordinary passport holders; a 2025 bilateral agreement covers only diplomatic/service/special passport holders.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit (excluding transit visa), issued by an EU Member State or Schengen Agreement state

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid residence permit or valid visa (excluding transit visas) issued by one of the Member States of the European Union or one of the States Parties to the Schengen Agreement, by the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the United States of America, or Canada may travel to the Republic of Moldova during the validity of that document. Applies to nationals listed under Footnote [2] of the official MFA visa regime page.

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen visa (Type C or Type D), excluding transit visas

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid Schengen-area visa (Type C short-stay or Type D national/long-stay), excluding transit visas, issued by any Schengen Agreement state may travel to Moldova during the visa's validity. Applies to nationals listed under Footnote [2] of the official MFA visa regime page.

credential.subtype: any valid UK visa or residence permit (excluding transit visas)

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid UK visa or UK residence permit (excluding transit visas), issued by the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, may travel to Moldova during the document's validity. Applies to nationals listed under Footnote [2] of the official MFA visa regime page.

credential.subtype: any valid US visa (excluding transit/C visas)

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid United States visa (excluding transit visas), issued by the United States of America, may travel to Moldova during the visa's validity. Applies to nationals listed under Footnote [2] of the official MFA visa regime page.

credential.subtype: any valid Canadian visa or residence permit (excluding transit visas)

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid Canadian visa or Canadian residence permit (excluding transit visas), issued by Canada, may travel to Moldova during the document's validity. Applies to nationals listed under Footnote [2] of the official MFA visa regime page.

Footnote [1] on the official MFA page: ordinary passport holders of these countries appear in the visa-REQUIRED section and need a visa or e-visa to enter Moldova. Only holders of diplomatic, service and special passports retain a treaty-based visa exemption under international agreements in force at the time the relevant Moldovan law was adopted. Max stay not specified on official source.

Diplomatic and service passport holders of these countries are exempt from Moldova visa requirements for up to 90 days within any 6-month period, based on bilateral visa waiver agreements. Ordinary passport holders of these countries are separately covered by unconditional visa-free access.

Diplomatic and service passport holders of China, Iran and Turkmenistan are exempt from Moldova visa requirements for up to 30 days, based on bilateral agreements. Ordinary passport holders of these countries require a visa (China is Footnote [2] - credential-based conditional access; Iran/Turkmenistan are Footnote [1] - must obtain a visa unless diplomatic/service/special passport).

Holders of Indian diplomatic and official passports are exempt from Moldova visa requirements under the Agreement on Visa Waiver for Holders of Diplomatic and Official Passports, signed 10 May 2024, entered into force 1 August 2024. Ordinary Indian passport holders remain subject to Footnote [2] conditions (visa-free only if holding valid EU/Schengen/UK/US/Canada visa or residence permit).

Holders of Israeli diplomatic and service passports are exempt from Moldova visa requirements under the Agreement on Waiver of Visa Requirement for Holders of Diplomatic and Service Passports, signed 22 June 1997 in Jerusalem, entered into force 15 February 1998. Ordinary Israeli passport holders have unconditional visa-free access.

Holders of Moroccan diplomatic, service, and special passports are exempt from Moldova visa requirements for up to 90 days within any 180-day period, under the bilateral visa waiver agreement for holders of diplomatic, service and special passports signed in Chisinau on 15 April 2025 and ratified by Moldovan Parliament in May 2025. Ordinary Moroccan passport holders require a visa (appear in visa-required section of MFA page).

Special Administrative Region passport holders only. Entry is permitted for holders of Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR travel documents (Special Administrative Region passports issued by the respective SAR governments). Holders of PRC ordinary passports are covered by Footnote [2] (credential-based conditional access), not by this grant.

cbi.program_name: Moldova Citizenship by Investment Program (MCBI) - REPEALED
cbi.notes: The MCBI program was launched in 2018 (Government Decision no. 786 of 04/10/2017) with a single option: non-reimbursable contribution to the Public Sustainable Development Fund (minimum €100,000 for primary applicant, €15,000 per additional family member). The program was suspended by parliamentary moratorium on 23 August 2019 and formally repealed on 1 September 2020 (Parliament voted 20 September 2020 with 80 of 101 MPs in favour). The repeal was partly a condition for EU macro-financial aid. As of 2026, no CBI program exists in Moldova.

programs.name: Temporary Residence Permit through Investment (Entrepreneurship/Investment)
programs.notes: Based on Law no. 200/2010 on the Regime of Foreigners and Law No 81/2004 on investments. Investment tiers tied to multiples of average monthly salary (AMS): 30–200 AMS or 1+ job = 2-year permit; 200–600 AMS or 8+ jobs = 3-year permit; 600–2000 AMS or 15+ jobs = 4-year permit; 2000–4000 AMS or 25+ jobs = 5-year permit; 4000+ AMS or 50+ jobs = 8-year permit. Application fee: 3,600 MDL. Permanent residence requires 5 years of temporary residence (other categories). No specific EUR/USD floor stated - amounts are in multiples of the national average salary, recalculated annually.

programs.name: Digital Nomad Visa (Remote Work Residence Permit)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals working remotely for foreign companies (employees, independent contractors, or shareholders/partners of foreign companies). Minimum income: 18 forecasted average salaries of Moldova per year for the preceding 6 months (approximately 3x national average monthly salary per month; estimated ~€2,700–2,800/month for 2026). Valid health insurance and proof of accommodation required.
programs.notes: Introduced via Law 144/2025, entered into force 20 September 2025. Administered by General Inspectorate for Migration (IGM) under Ministry of Internal Affairs. Duration: up to 2 years (initial period up to 2 years, renewable following the same procedure). Government fee governed by Law on state fees no. 213 of 31.07.2023. Applications: in person at IGM Chisinau or via representative. Income must derive exclusively from remote work for foreign entities.

programs.name: Long-Stay Visa Type D/IT (IT Activities)
programs.eligibility: Technology specialists intending to carry out IT activities in Moldova.
programs.processing_time: Standard consular processing (not specified in days on official source)
programs.notes: Long-stay Type D visa subtype for IT specialists. Allows entry and stay up to 90 days, after which temporary residence permit may be required.

agreements: CIS Free Trade Area (member - Commonwealth of Independent States; note: Moldova denounced the 1992 CIS Bishkek visa-free travel agreement in November 2025, effective for Tajikistan and Kyrgyzstan only as bilateral agreements cover all other CIS states)

validation_notes: CORRECTION 2026-06-14: Indonesia removed from visa_policy.visa_free - it is a Footnote [1] country on the MFA page, meaning ordinary passport holders are in the visa-REQUIRED section. Only diplomatic/service/special passport holders have a treaty-based exemption. Indonesia now correctly appears in conditional_access (passport_type basis).

CORRECTION 2026-06-14: El Salvador added to visa_policy.visa_free - it was missing from the prior record despite being confirmed as unconditional visa-free on the MFA page (listed as 'Republic of El Salvador' with no footnote).

CORRECTION 2026-06-14: China added to all five Footnote [2] conditional_access entries - the MFA page lists China as 'People's Republic of China [2]' under visa-required but credential-eligible. The prior record only had China in a diplomatic/service passport_type entry (30-day bilateral), which remains and is correct, but ordinary PRC passport holders with qualifying credentials were not captured.

CORRECTION 2026-06-14: Cuba, Swaziland (Eswatini), Sao Tome and Principe, and Comoros added to all five Footnote [2] conditional_access eligible_nationalities arrays - these countries appear as '[2]' on the MFA visa regime page but were absent from the prior record's conditional_access lists.

CORRECTION 2026-06-14: Footnote [1] conditional_access entry consolidated - prior record had Iran and Turkmenistan only in the diplomatic/service 30-day bilateral entry; Egypt, Indonesia, Vietnam are also Footnote [1] countries (ordinary passports need a visa). A new unified passport_type entry for Egypt, Indonesia, Iran, Turkmenistan, Vietnam added.

CORRECTION 2026-06-14: CIS Bishkek Agreement denunciation noted - Moldova's government approved denunciation of the 1992 CIS visa-free travel agreement on 12 November 2025, with parliament approving in first reading 27 November 2025. This affects Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan (no bilateral agreements with Moldova). MFA visa regime page still shows both as visa-free as of June 2026; added cautionary notes to their entries.

CORRECTION 2026-06-14: Digital Nomad program entry into force date corrected to '20 September 2025' (Law 144/2025) from the vague 'September 2025' in the prior record. Also clarified that the permit duration is up to 2 years (not '1 year renewable once') per invest.gov.md.

VALIDATION 2026-06-27: Full re-fetch of mfa.gov.md/en/content/visa-regime-foreigners confirmed the visa-free list is unchanged - all nationalities in visa_policy.visa_free remain as listed, with no additions or removals. Morocco confirmed visa-free (no footnote) on MFA page - already correctly recorded. Dominican Republic confirmed visa-required with footnote [2] - already correctly absent from visa_free and present in conditional_access. Footnote [2] text confirmed: exempts holders of EU/Schengen/UK/US/Canada residence permits or valid visas (excluding transit visas). Footnote [1] text confirmed: exempts holders of diplomatic/service/special passports who traveled visa-free at time of law adoption under existing bilateral treaties.

VALIDATION 2026-06-27: CIS Bishkek Agreement denunciation update - parliament gave full (final) approval on 12 December 2025 (not just first reading on 27 November 2025). MFA official page as of 27 June 2026 still shows Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan as visa-free. Record preserved as-is per safety rule (official page still lists them). Effective date of visa requirement remains unpublished on official sources.

VALIDATION 2026-06-27: Suriname confirmed visa-required with no footnote exemption - directly confirmed from official MFA visa-regime page re-fetch. Suriname appears in the visa-required section without [1] or [2] footnotes, meaning ordinary Surinamese passport holders need a standard visa with no credential-based shortcut. Gap entry updated accordingly.

CORRECTION 2026-06-29: Morocco REMOVED from visa_policy.visa_free. Re-fetch of mfa.gov.md/en/content/visa-regime-foreigners confirmed Morocco does NOT appear in the unconditional visa-free list for ordinary passports; it is in the visa-required section. The May 2025 Moldova-Morocco bilateral agreement (signed Chisinau 15 April 2025, ratified by Moldovan Parliament May 2025) covers ONLY diplomatic, service, and special passport holders. Ordinary Moroccan passport holders require a visa. Morocco added to conditional_access with passport_type basis (diplomatic/service/special, 90 days). Prior visa-free entry was erroneous.
gaps: The MFA official page (mfa.gov.md/en/content/visa-regime-foreigners) does not explicitly state max_stay_days on the page itself; the 90-day/180-day figure is confirmed via the MFA embassy sub-pages and evisa.gov.md portal description.

The e-visa eligible country list is not enumerated on the official evisa.gov.md page (SSL certificate error prevented direct scrape on 2026-06-27 re-check); the egov.md official portal confirms the system exists but does not list eligible nationalities.

Specific EUR/USD equivalent investment minimums for RBI investment tiers are not stated on official sources - amounts are expressed as multiples of the national average monthly salary, updated annually.
The CBI program official domain cbi.gov.md had an expired SSL certificate and could not be scraped; program closure confirmed via multiple secondary sources referencing official parliamentary action.

No official page enumerates specific nationalities eligible for Moldova e-visa vs. those who must apply through an embassy; this distinction exists but is not fully documented in official English sources found.

The full list of Footnote [2] nationalities was confirmed from multiple fetches of the MFA visa regime page. Secondary aggregator sources also mentioned Bolivia (included), Cuba (now added), Sao Tome and Principe (now added), Comoros (now added), and Swaziland (now added).

Moldova reports having approximately 45 bilateral diplomatic/service passport visa waiver agreements (per MFA statements). Only those confirmed directly from official mfa.gov.md sub-pages or embassy pages are recorded. A complete consolidated treaty list on the official MFA website was not accessible.

OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder entry rules for Moldova: no official Moldovan government page found that specifically addresses OCI cardholders as a separate category. No conditional_access entry recorded for OCI basis.

Exact effective date for Moldova's visa requirement against Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan (following CIS Bishkek Agreement denunciation - final parliamentary approval 12 December 2025) is not published on any official source found. MFA visa regime page still lists both as visa-free as of 27 June 2026.
The Digital Nomad program fee amount is not confirmed on the official invest.gov.md page; fees are governed by Law 213/2023 which was not scraped. Specific fee amount not recorded.
Gabon appears in Footnote [2] list on the MFA page and is confirmed correctly recorded in conditional_access.

Suriname RESOLVED: confirmed visa-required with no footnote exemption on official MFA page re-fetch 2026-06-27. Surinamese ordinary passport holders must obtain a standard visa; no credential-based exemption applies.

Morocco RESOLVED 2026-06-29: confirmed visa-required for ordinary passports. Removed from visa_free; added to conditional_access for diplomatic/service/special passports only (2025 bilateral agreement). Exact date of entry into force of the 2025 diplomatic-passport agreement not confirmed from an official Moldovan government page (MFA news article/Morocco World News secondary source used for dating - April 15 signing, May 2025 ratification); confirm when official MFA bilateral-treaties page is accessible.

Dominican Republic dual-listing on MFA page investigated 2026-06-29: WebFetch of the MFA page returned contradictory information (appeared in both visa-free and visa-required[2] sections). Wikipedia article and other aggregators confirm Dominican Republic is NOT in the unconditional visa-free list. Record correctly has Dominican Republic only in conditional_access (Footnote [2]) — no change made. If MFA page is updated to clearly resolve this ambiguity, re-verify.

outbound_official.notes: Source page is in Romanian (mfa.gov.md/ro/content/regimul-de-vize-pentru-cetatenii-republicii-moldova), last updated 2026-02-20. The MFA notes information may change without prior notification and travelers should verify with destination authorities. EU/Schengen area (90 days/180-day period) accessible via visa liberalization agreement in force since 2014. Ireland listed only for diplomatic passport holders. Some destinations (e.g., Barbados 28 days, Dominica 21 days, Micronesia 30 days) have shorter stays than the standard 90 days.

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Visa Type C/T (Tourism)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals entering Moldova for tourist purposes, including visits to tourist attractions, recreation, and leisure activities.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires confirmed reservation at tourist accommodation, medical insurance of at least €30,000. Apply at Moldovan embassy/consulate or via e-visa portal at evisa.gov.md. 90 days within any 180-day period.

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Visa Type C/A (Business)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals entering Moldova for commercial or economic purposes, including negotiations, meetings, conferences, and trade activities.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires business invitation, proof of accommodation, and subsistence funds (minimum €30/day). Medical insurance required. 90 days within any 180-day period.

visa_types.name: Airport Transit Visa Type A
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals transiting through a Moldovan international airport without entering the country's territory.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €20 (approx USD 22). Requires onward air ticket for destination country and evidence of admission to the third country of destination. Only for international transit zone at airport.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa Type B
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals transiting through Moldova by land, sea, or air en route to a third country.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires valid travel ticket to destination country, proof of entry permission in destination country, and medical insurance of at least €30,000. Transit duration typically up to 5 days.

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Visa Type C/MI (Official Mission)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals traveling to Moldova on official government or public administration missions.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires verbal note from the sending government or public administration body. Valid passport issued within 10 years.

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Visa Type C/V (Private Visit)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals visiting family members or friends who are residents of Moldova.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires invitation from host resident, copy of host's identity document, and medical insurance. 90 days within any 180-day period.

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Visa Type C/TS (Transport)
visa_types.purpose: For professional drivers and transport workers engaged in freight or passenger transport through or to Moldova.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires transport license, vehicle registration documents, and proof of employment as transport professional.

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Visa Type C/S (Sports)
visa_types.purpose: For athletes, coaches, and sports officials participating in official sports competitions or events in Moldova.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires official sports delegation list, proof of accommodation, and medical insurance.

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Visa Type C/AD (Cultural/Scientific/Humanitarian)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals participating in cultural, scientific, academic, or humanitarian activities in Moldova.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires justification of the activity's purpose, proof of accommodation, and medical insurance.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa Type D/AI (Entrepreneurship/Investment)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals intending to establish or invest in a business in Moldova, including shareholders and company founders.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires proof of investments or business activity in Moldova, criminal record certificate, and medical insurance. Entry visa that precedes application for temporary residence permit based on investment/entrepreneurship.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa Type D/AM (Employment)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who have received authorization to work in Moldova, including seasonal workers.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires work authorization decision from Moldova's General Inspectorate for Migration (IGM), proof of subsistence, and criminal record certificate. Covers both permanent and seasonal employment.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa Type D/AS (Study)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals accepted to study at an educational institution in Moldova.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires confirmation of enrollment/approval to study from the educational institution and proof of financial capacity to support studies and stay.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa Type D/VF (Family Reunification)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals joining a spouse, children, or parents who hold a residence permit or are citizens of Moldova.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires acceptance for family reunification from Moldova's authorities and proof of family ties (marriage certificate, birth certificates, etc.).

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa Type D/RU (Religious/Humanitarian)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals conducting religious, charitable, or humanitarian volunteer work in Moldova.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires registration of the religious or charitable organization in Moldova, a volunteer/employment contract, and proof of accommodation.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa Type D/DS (Diplomatic/Service)
visa_types.purpose: For diplomatic staff, consular officers, and accredited international organization personnel and their family members.
visa_types.notes: No consular fee. Requires diplomatic passport and verbal note from the sending state. Issued to diplomatic mission staff and accredited international organization personnel posted to Moldova.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa Type D/TR (Medical Treatment)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals traveling to Moldova for long-term medical treatment at a medical institution.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires acceptance letter from the medical institution in Moldova, and proof of financial capacity to cover treatment costs.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa Type D/IT (IT Activities)
visa_types.purpose: For technology specialists intending to carry out IT activities, particularly within Moldova's IT Park system.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires employer declaration from an IT Park registered company and an employment decision. Entry visa preceding temporary residence permit application for IT activities in Moldova's IT Park.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa Type D/TP (Intra-Company Transfer)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign employees temporarily transferred to a branch, subsidiary, or affiliated entity in Moldova within the same company.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires confirmation of the intra-company transfer, copy of the legal entity's registration certificate in Moldova.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa Type D/FT (Internship/Professional Training)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals participating in professional development programs, internships, or vocational training at a Moldovan organization.
visa_types.notes: Fee is €40 (approx USD 43). Requires confirmation from the legal entity in Moldova of the internship/training purpose and program details.

visa_types.name: Digital Nomad Residence Permit (Remote Work)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who work remotely for foreign companies or clients while residing in Moldova.
visa_types.notes: Introduced via Law 144/2025, in force 20 September 2025. This is a residence permit (not a visa). Applicants must demonstrate minimum income of 18 times Moldova's forecasted average salary per year for the preceding 6 months (approx €2,700-2,800/month as of 2026). Income must derive exclusively from remote work for foreign entities - as employee, independent contractor, or shareholder/partner. Valid health insurance and proof of accommodation required. Apply in person at General Inspectorate for Migration (IGM) in Chisinau. Duration up to 2 years, renewable. Fee governed by Law on state fees no. 213 of 31.07.2023 (exact amount not published on official source).
